title: From Desacralization To Resacralization Of Power
text:
From Desacralization To Resacralization Of Power

Didier Darcet

Gavekal-IS | 25 Jun 2026

The history of politics can be read as a long struggle to strip power of its sacred aura. For millennia, to rule was to embody the divine: kings governed by heavenly mandate, emperors stood as intermediaries between men and gods, authority drew its legitimacy from the sacred. Over time, however, power slowly separated itself from the sacred, culminating in the rise of modern democracies, where authority no longer descended from heaven but rose from the people.Yet contemporary capitalism may be turning this trajectory on its head. After centuries of desacralization, recent decades suggest power may be acquiring a sacred character again, this time in economic form.
